steps.title: Where does the agent run? (infra fork)
Which product shape do you optimize for?

options.label: Generic OSS runtime (e.g. Ollama) + DIY glue
Hosted API SaaS only (no curated local stack)

steps.title: Future scenario: how do bots pay each other? (design only)

options.label: No money rail — internal compute metering only

outcomes.title: Friction for continuous agent economy
outcomes.body: Cloud API + fiat-only + strict KYC: a poor fit for machine-to-machine micropayments without repeated human approvals.

outcomes.title: Aligned with QVAC + USDT horizon (demo)
outcomes.body: Local-first + QVAC-shaped suite + USDT between bots in a sandbox policy. Legal and issuer constraints still map to roadmap work.

outcomes.title: Local OSS without a payment rail
outcomes.body: Great for privacy and prototypes; lacks the unified micropayment / agent-economy story QVAC can channel over time.

outcomes.title: Stablecoins under heavy gating
outcomes.body: Stablecoin + heavy compliance fits regulated B2B; agent autonomy often narrows to known counterparties.

defaultOutcome.title: Hybrid mix
defaultOutcome.body: Selections suggest partly automatable flows with policy exceptions or manual edges — a typical cross-team implementation backlog.
